seoTitle: InventChip Products | SiC MOSFETs, Power Modules | BeiLuo
seoDescription: Browse InventChip SiC MOSFETs and power modules. Technical support for EV, solar, and industrial applications in China market.

faqs.question: What product categories does InventChip offer?
faqs.answer: InventChip offers four main product categories: SiC MOSFETs featuring planar and trench technology with voltage ratings from 650V to 1700V; SiC Power Modules including half-bridge and full-bridge configurations for high-power applications; SiC Bare Die for customers requiring custom module assembly; and Gate Drivers optimized for SiC device operation. Each category provides cost-effective solutions without compromising quality or performance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Browse our product categories below or contact our FAE team for product selection assistance.

faqs.question: How does InventChip compare to international SiC brands?
faqs.answer: InventChip compares favorably to international brands: (1) Performance - Comparable electrical characteristics. (2) Quality - Same automotive certifications (AEC-Q101). (3) Price - 20-30% lower cost. (4) Support - Local China FAE team. (5) Supply - Stable domestic manufacturing. (6) Innovation - Continuous R&D investment. (7) Applications - Proven in EV, solar, industrial. Excellent alternative to Wolfspeed, Infineon, and Rohm.
faqs.decisionGuide: InventChip provides international-quality SiC with local support and competitive pricing.

faqs.question: What are the key advantages of InventChip SiC devices?
faqs.answer: InventChip SiC advantages: (1) Cost-effective - Competitive pricing. (2) Quality - International certifications. (3) Automotive - AEC-Q101 qualified. (4) Local support - China-based FAE team. (5) Supply security - Domestic manufacturing. (6) Performance - Comparable to international brands. (7) Innovation - Continuous R&D. InventChip delivers excellent value for SiC solutions.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose InventChip for cost-effective SiC with quality and local support.

categories.name: SiC MOSFETs
categories.description: InventChip SiC MOSFETs with planar and trench technology, voltage ratings from 650V to 1700V for EV, solar, and industrial applications.

products.name: 1200V 40mΩ SiC MOSFET
products.shortDescription: 1200V 40mΩ SiC MOSFET in TO-247-3 package for high-power applications
descriptionParagraphs: The IV1D12040T3 is a 1200V, 40mΩ SiC MOSFET designed for high-power applications requiring excellent switching performance and low conduction losses. This device features planar SiC technology with robust gate oxide reliability.

With a typical on-resistance of 40mΩ at 25°C, this MOSFET delivers excellent conduction performance while maintaining fast switching characteristics. The device is suitable for 800V EV traction inverters, solar inverters, and industrial power supplies.
The TO-247-3 package provides excellent thermal performance and is compatible with standard mounting hardware. The device is fully AEC-Q101 qualified for automotive applications.
features: Fast switching with low switching losses

faeReview.author: Li Wei
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Automotive
faeReview.content: The IV1D12040T3 is our most popular 1200V device for EV applications. Customers consistently achieve 98%+ inverter efficiency with this device. The 40mΩ Rds(on) provides an excellent balance of conduction and switching losses. For 800V EV traction inverters, this is the go-to device. The AEC-Q101 qualification and competitive pricing make it ideal for automotive applications.
faeReview.highlight: Best-selling 1200V device for EV traction inverters

faqs.question: What is the typical gate drive voltage for IV1D12040T3?
faqs.answer: Recommended gate drive: +18V for turn-on and -5V for turn-off. This provides optimal switching performance and noise immunity. The gate threshold voltage is typically 2.5V. Use a gate resistor of 5-10Ω to control switching speed and EMI.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use +18V/-5V gate drive with 5-10Ω gate resistor for optimal performance.

faqs.question: How do I calculate switching losses for IV1D12040T3?
faqs.answer: Switching losses = Eon × fsw + Eoff × fsw. At 800V/40A: Eon ≈ 1.2mJ, Eoff ≈ 0.8mJ. For 50kHz switching: Psw = (1.2+0.8)mJ × 50kHz = 100W. Conduction loss: Pcond = I² × Rds(on) × duty cycle. Total losses = Psw + Pcond. Use thermal calculator for junction temperature.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use loss calculator tools or contact FAE for detailed analysis.

products.name: 650V 80mΩ SiC MOSFET
products.shortDescription: 650V 80mΩ SiC MOSFET in TO-247-3 package for 400V systems
descriptionParagraphs: The IV1D06580T3 is a 650V, 80mΩ SiC MOSFET optimized for 400V applications such as onboard chargers, DC-DC converters, and industrial power supplies. This device offers excellent performance at a competitive price point.

With a typical on-resistance of 80mΩ at 25°C, this MOSFET provides efficient power conversion for medium-power applications. The 650V rating is ideal for 400V EV systems and general industrial applications.

The device features fast switching characteristics that enable high-frequency operation, reducing passive component size and system cost. The TO-247-3 package provides good thermal performance and ease of mounting.

faeReview.author: Zhang Ming
faeReview.title: FAE - Power Electronics
faeReview.content: The IV1D06580T3 is an excellent choice for 400V onboard chargers and DC-DC converters. The 80mΩ Rds(on) provides good efficiency at a very competitive price. Customers report 20-30% cost savings compared to international brands with comparable performance. The device is easy to drive and works well with standard gate drivers.
faeReview.highlight: Cost-effective solution for 400V OBC and DC-DC

faqs.question: Is IV1D06580T3 suitable for automotive OBC applications?
faqs.answer: Yes, the IV1D06580T3 is suitable for automotive OBC applications. While not specifically AEC-Q101 qualified (check latest datasheet), it meets industrial reliability standards. For automotive production, consider using the automotive-qualified variants or contact InventChip for PPAP support.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact FAE for automotive qualification status and PPAP support.

products.name: 1200V 20mΩ SiC MOSFET
products.shortDescription: 1200V 20mΩ SiC MOSFET for high-power EV traction inverters
descriptionParagraphs: The IV1D12020T3 is a high-performance 1200V, 20mΩ SiC MOSFET designed for high-power EV traction inverters and industrial motor drives. The ultra-low on-resistance minimizes conduction losses for maximum efficiency.

With only 20mΩ Rds(on), this device can handle high continuous currents while maintaining excellent thermal performance. The device is ideal for 150kW+ EV traction inverters and high-power industrial applications.
The TO-247-3 package with Kelvin source connection enables optimal switching performance by eliminating common source inductance effects.

faeReview.author: Li Wei
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Automotive
faeReview.content: The IV1D12020T3 is our highest current 1200V device, perfect for high-power EV traction inverters. The 20mΩ Rds(on) enables 150kW+ inverter designs with excellent efficiency. The Kelvin source connection is critical for high-current switching performance. This device competes directly with Wolfspeed C3M0021120K at a significantly lower price point.

faqs.question: What is the maximum power for an inverter using IV1D12020T3?
faqs.answer: For a 3-phase inverter with 6x IV1D12020T3: At 800V DC bus, 150A RMS current, the inverter can deliver approximately 180kW continuous power. Peak power can reach 250kW+ for short durations. Efficiency typically exceeds 98.5% at full load.

products.name: 1200V 30mΩ Gen2 SiC MOSFET
products.shortDescription: Second generation 1200V 30mΩ SiC MOSFET with improved performance
descriptionParagraphs: The IV2D12030T3 is a second-generation 1200V SiC MOSFET featuring trench technology for improved performance. With 30mΩ Rds(on), this device offers an excellent balance of conduction and switching losses.
The Gen2 trench technology provides better channel mobility and reduced on-resistance compared to planar devices. This results in lower conduction losses and improved overall efficiency.
The device is suitable for a wide range of applications including EV traction inverters, solar inverters, and industrial motor drives where high efficiency is critical.

faeReview.author: Li Wei
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Automotive
faeReview.content: The IV2D12030T3 with Gen2 trench technology represents InventChip's latest advancement. The 30mΩ Rds(on) hits a sweet spot for many EV applications. The improved trench technology provides better performance than planar devices at a competitive price. This is becoming a popular choice for next-generation EV inverter designs.
faeReview.highlight: Gen2 trench technology for improved performance

faqs.question: What is the difference between Gen1 and Gen2 InventChip devices?
faqs.answer: Gen2 devices use trench gate technology vs planar for Gen1. Benefits: (1) Lower Rds(on) for same die size. (2) Better channel mobility. (3) Improved switching performance. (4) Lower gate charge. (5) Better figure-of-merit. Gen2 is recommended for new designs requiring best performance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose Gen2 for best performance; Gen1 for cost optimization.

categories.name: SiC Power Modules
categories.description: InventChip SiC power modules including half-bridge and full-bridge configurations for high-power EV and industrial applications.

products.name: 1200V Half-Bridge Module
products.shortDescription: 1200V half-bridge SiC power module with 40mΩ MOSFETs
descriptionParagraphs: The IVM12040HB is a 1200V half-bridge SiC power module featuring two 40mΩ SiC MOSFETs in a compact, low-inductance package. This module is ideal for EV traction inverters and high-power motor drives.
The module integrates high-performance SiC MOSFETs with optimized internal layout to minimize parasitic inductance. This enables fast switching with low overshoot and reduced EMI.
The standard package footprint allows for easy integration into existing designs. The module is fully tested and characterized for reliable operation.

faeReview.author: Li Wei
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Automotive
faeReview.content: The IVM12040HB module simplifies EV traction inverter design by integrating two switches with optimized layout. The low inductance design enables clean switching waveforms. Customers appreciate the pre-tested modules that reduce development time. Contact FAE for module application support and thermal design guidelines.
faeReview.highlight: Integrated half-bridge for simplified inverter design

faqs.question: What is the advantage of using a module vs discrete devices?
faqs.answer: Module advantages: (1) Lower parasitic inductance - better switching. (2) Pre-tested - known good. (3) Thermal management - optimized design. (4) Assembly - fewer components. (5) Reliability - qualified module. Discrete advantages: (1) Flexibility - individual device replacement. (2) Cost - lower for high volume. (3) Layout - customizable. Choose based on volume and design requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use modules for >50kW; discrete for <50kW or high volume.
